frameworks.name: distributed semantic vector space
frameworks.one_line: words and sentences represented as vectors from massive text corpora

frameworks.name: brain decoding (linear mapping from fMRI to semantic vectors)
frameworks.one_line: learn map from imaging features to semantic vector targets

frameworks.name: compositional sentence meaning
frameworks.one_line: extends concrete-noun decoding to sentence-level abstract meaning

core_question: Can a brain decoder generalize from individual-word imaging data to sentence meanings spanning concrete and abstract topics?
key_assumptions: linguistic meaning is captured by vectors in a corpus-derived semantic space
sampling the semantic space efficiently maximizes generalization from limited fMRI data
training on individual concepts transfers to sentence-level meaning
manipulations: training stimuli selected to efficiently sample the semantic vector space
testing across two separate fMRI datasets
varying stimulus content from concrete to abstract topics
key_findings: decoder trained on individual concepts decodes semantic vectors for whole sentences
generalizes across two separate datasets and to abstract topics
decoded representations distinguish even semantically similar sentences
decoded representations capture similarity structure between sentence meanings
interpretations: corpus-derived semantic vectors plus efficient stimulus sampling enable a universal-style decoder of meaning from brain activity
